name: translator-starter
title: Translator Starter
description: Paste text, get it back in TARGET_LANGUAGE. Instructions-only вЂ” the entire product is the prompt.

files.content:
# Language to translate into. Read when eve compiles the agent (eve dev / build).
TARGET_LANGUAGE=Spanish

# Model access follows your eve setup (Vercel AI Gateway or a provider key).

# Translator Starter

Paste text, get it back in your target language. Instructions-only вЂ” no tools, no channels required (uses the default eve HTTP / dev chat surface).

One env var (`TARGET_LANGUAGE`). Demos: instructions as the entire product.

## Layout

```text
translator-starter/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ package.json
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agent/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agent.ts
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ instructions.ts   # в†ђ TARGET_LANGUAGE baked in at compile time
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ evals/
```

## Run

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Type any English sentence in the dev terminal; the reply is Spanish by default. Change language:

```bash
TARGET_LANGUAGE=French npm run dev
```

## Make it yours

Set `TARGET_LANGUAGE`, or edit `agent/instructions.ts` for tone rules (formal, slang, preserve brand names, etc.).

## Verify

```bash
npm run eval
```

## License

MIT

# Set up Translator Starter

## 1. Install and run

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env   # optional вЂ” defaults to Spanish
npm run dev
```

`TARGET_LANGUAGE` is read when the agent compiles. Restart `eve dev` after changing it.

## 2. Optional: add a channel

This starter has no channel file. Point Telegram, Slack, or Discord at it the same way as the other starters if you want a mobile or workspace surface.

## Verify

```bash
npm run eval
```

# Example request

Good morning, how are you?

# Expected behavior

A natural greeting in the target language (default Spanish) with no preface or explanation вЂ” translation only.
